Attack cards

Weapons are used by the character to fend off enemies, each having its own unique way of doing so. Most weapons deal damage to enemies, while some apply debuffs to enemies or buffs to the player.

The player can select up to six different weapons from level-ups, but it is possible to carry more by picking up weapons that generate with the stage. Most weapons must be unlocked before they can be offered to the player.

Most weapons are unique, which means a character can only gain a weapon once per run, and there is no way to get the same weapon multiple times (even if it is consumed). Exceptions include single-use weapons like  Candybox or  Arma Dio.

Obtaining

Normally, each character has access to one of the base weapons as their starting weapon and is able to obtain additional base weapons as they level up. Rarity determines the weight of how often that weapon shows up in the pool of possible weapons and passive items during the level-up screen and when opening  Treasure Chests.

Upgrading

There is a certain chance for weapons that the player has already owned to be offered to them upon level-up. If an already owned weapon is selected, it will be upgraded to a higher level, and it will gain certain stat bonuses unique to the weapon. When a weapon is fully leveled, it cannot appear in the level-up options anymore. Upgrading a weapon will also reset its  Cooldown and activate it instantly, which is useful for powerful weapons with long Cooldown.

Evolution

Main article: Evolution

When a base weapon is fully leveled and its corresponding passive item is obtained, it can be transformed into an evolved weapon by collecting a  Treasure Chest. Some weapons only need to be fully leveled for them to evolve. Not all weapons have an evolution.

Evolution actually works by removing the base weapon and acquiring the evolved one (with a few exceptions), which means whenever a weapon is evolved, all the effects of the original weapon will be lost, and the evolved weapon's Cooldown starts from zero (which is noticeable for weapons with long Cooldown, like Santa Water). Evolved weapons also remove the base weapon regardless of whether obtained by normal evolution rules or not (for example, obtaining an evolution by  Super Candybox II Turbo will also remove its base weapon).

Certain characters ( Mortaccio,  Yatta Cavallo,  Bianca Ramba, and  O'Sole Meeo) automatically evolve their starting weapon when they morph into a new form, provided that the player has obtained the required relic and met the necessary condition.

Stats

Weapons have different stats, which define their functions.

See Overview Stats for a list of base stats of the weapons.

Weapon Stats
Max Level The highest level the weapon can be upgraded to. Rarity The weight of the weapon in the pool of weapons and passive items.
Base Damage The damage dealt by a single projectile per hit. Modified by Might stat.[1] Area The base area of the weapon. Modified by Area stat.
Speed The base projectile speed of the weapon. Modified by Speed stat. Amount The amount of projectiles fired per use. Modified by Amount stat.
Duration The duration of the weapon's effect. Modified by Duration stat. Pierce The number of enemies a single projectile can hit before being used up.
Cooldown The time required for the weapon to be used again. There is a trigger for the cooldown to start after a weapon's duration ends. Modified by Cooldown stat. Projectile Interval The time required for an additional projectile to be fired between each Cooldown. Can overlap with another set of shots even before it finishes shooting.
Hitbox Delay The time before the same enemy can be hit by the same projectile again. All delays refresh simultaneously as soon as one fully counts down. Knockback The knockback dealt multiplier of the weapon. Determines the strength of the knockback effect along with the enemy's knockback taken multiplier and movement speed.
Pool Limit The maximum amount of projectiles that can be on screen. Chance The chance of a special effect happening. Modified by Luck stat. Technically, Crit Chance is a separate stat but both have not been used at the same time yet.
Crit Multi Damage multiplier applied to critical hits. Block by Walls Can the projectile be blocked by obstacles.
  1. ↑ The damage displayed with the "Damage Numbers" option ticked shows a randomized value within ±5 of the scaled base damage, but this is only a visual feature and the damage dealt by a weapon is always the same.

Types

There are base, evolutions or unions, and special weapons.

See Combos for a list of which passive items and their corresponding player stats affect which weapon.

Base Weapons

Base weapons can be chosen by leveling up. Most of them are locked behind achievements at first.

Evolutions

Traditionally, evolved weapons cannot be upgraded, only have one level, and have a rarity of 1. However, newer expansion content such as the Emerald Diorama breaks many of the older conventions for weapon evolutions. Notable exceptions of older weapons that broke these conventions are the Bi-Bracelet, Tri-Bracelet, Alucard Swords, Vol Confodere, Melio Confodere, Vol Luminatio, and Vol Umbra. Many newer weapons also have additional secondary evolutions.

Icon Weapon Description Base Damage General Information Evolve From Requirements Belongs To

Unions

Union weapons are a unique type of evolution. They merge two weapons into one and free up a weapon slot. They may or may not need a passive item to be united.

Icon Weapon Description Base Damage General Information Requirements Belongs To
Vandalier Union of Ebony Wings and Peachone. 28
  • Starting weapon of  Smith IV
  • Acquisition will free a weapon slot
  • Has two areas that orbit in both directions
  • Has a max Level of 8
  • Scales with all stat bonuses and Move Speed
  • Fully level  Peachone and  Ebony Wings
  Super Candybox II Turbo
Phieraggi Union of Phiera Der Tuphello and Eight The Sparrow. Scales with Revivals. 15
  • Ignores Duration
  • Acquisition will free a weapon slot
  • Scales with Revival
  • Fully level  Phiera Der Tuphello and  Eight The Sparrow
  • Requires  Tirajisú for evolution
  Super Candybox II Turbo
Fuwalafuwaloo Union of Vento Sacro and Bloody Tear. Critical hits might generate explosions 20
  • Starting weapon of  Rose De Infernas
  • Scales with all stat bonuses, MoveSpeed, and Luck
  • Max Level  Vento Sacro and  Bloody Tear
  Super Candybox II Turbo
SpellStrom Union of SpellString, SpellStream, and SpellStrike. Grows stronger after every singularity. 15
  • Scales with all stat bonuses
  • Fully level  SpellString,  SpellStream, and  SpellStrike
  Super Candybox II Turbo
Trinum Custodem Union of Dextro Custos, Sinestro Custos, and Centralis Custos. 60
  • Starting weapon of  Blue Crescent Moon Cornell 
  • Ignores Speed and Duration
  • Fully level  Dextro Custos,  Sinestro Custos, and  Centralis Custos
  Super Candybox II Turbo
Power of Sire Union of Dominus Anger, Dominus Hatred and Dominus Agony. Deals critical damage to anything in sight. Drains health. 60
  • Fully level  Dominus Anger,  Dominus Hatred, and  Dominus Agony
  Super Candybox II Turbo
Million Cut Union of Valmanway and Thousand Edge. Slashes endlessly. Grows bigger with continuous movement. 33
  • Max Level  Valmanway and  Thousand Edge
Ninth Circle Union of Icebrand and Unholy Vespers. Hell truly has frozen over. 45
  • Max Level  Icebrand and  Unholy Vespers
Dies Irae Union of Arrow of Goth and Mannajja. Alamaric Snipers rain down arrows, even more so on critical hit. 20
  • Max Level  Arrow of Goth and  Mannajja
Kardía Phlegeton Union of Aura Blast and Hellfire. Calls forth a massive volcanic eruption. 40
  • Max Level  Aura Blast and  Hellfire
Lapiste Tepisto Union of Kaiser Knuckle and Tri-Bracelet. Launches a barrage of stony fists. 60
  • Max Level  Kaiser Knuckle and  Tri-Bracelet
Darkness Illusion Union of Svarog Statue and Valkyrie Turner. Crimson clones dance with you in the flames. 35
  • Max Level  Svarog Statue and  Valkyrie Turner
Carnage Heart Union of Troll Bomb and NO FUTURE. This one does in fact bounce. 30
  • Max Level  Troll Bomb and  NO FUTURE
Hydro Pump Climax Union of Hydro Storm and La Borra. It never rains but it pours. 20
  • Max Level  Hydro Storm and  La Borra
Arch Angle Union of Grand Cross and Heaven Sword. Central beam pulses when crosses pass through it. 77
  • Max Level  Grand Cross and  Heaven Sword
Spirit of Light Union of Summon Spirit and Holy Wand. Fires homing spirits from a portal of light. 60
  • Max Level  Summon Spirit and  Holy Wand
Power of Lire Union of Soul Steal and Vicious Hunger. Devours enemies with insatiable greed. 10
  • Max Level  Soul Steal and  Vicious Hunger
Legacy of Death: Soul River Union of Dark Rift and Death Spiral. Death becomes you. 80
  • Max Level  Dark Rift and  Death Spiral
Vjaya Sisters Union of Sword Brothers and Thunder Loop. Lightning triggers sword explosions with brief Invulnerability. 100
  • Max Level  Sword Brothers and  Thunder Loop
Venus Crescent Union of Summon Spirit Tornado and Gorgeous Moon. Love, Beauty and Light. N/A
  • Max Level  Summon Spirit Tornado and  Gorgeous Moon
Dark Frogamorphosis Union of Anura and Soul Eater. May actually eat enemies. 30
  • Max Level  Anura and  Soul Eater
Clock Tower Union of Endo Gears, Peri Pendulum, Myo Lift, and Epi Heads. 10
  • Fully level  Endo Gears,  Peri Pendulum,  Myo Lift and  Epi Head

Gifts

Gifts are extra weapons or passive items which can only be obtained via Treasure Chest when player meets certain conditions. Most gifts do not replace the base weapon or passive item.

Icon Weapon Description Base Damage General Information Requirements
Super Candybox II Turbo Gift of Candybox. Allows to choose among a selection of advanced weapons. N/A
  • Has a rarity of 0.1
  • Rarity affected by Luck
  • Cannot choose certain weapons like Infinite Corridor, Crimson Shroud and Ashes of Muspell
  • Requires having at least 1  Candybox
Sole Solution Gift of Victory Sword. The more enemies are defeated, the stronger it grows. 10
  • Ignores everything but Might
  • Requires both  Victory Sword and  Torrona's Box at max Level to obtain this gift
Alucard Shield Gift of Alucard Sword. Absorbs and triggers all other equipped evolutions. Hovering shield damages enemies. 255
  • Functions as a weapon and take a weapon slot
  • Requires any 6 evolutions and 6 passive items at max Level
Universitas Gift of Vol Luminatio and Vol Umbra. Meteors rain down on enemies at fixed intervals. 50
  • Requires  Vol Luminatio and  Vol Umbra at max Level

Counterpart Weapons

Counterpart weapons cannot appear when leveling up. They are used as the counterpart of an existing weapon when selecting the Gemini (I) Arcana.

Icon Weapon Description Base Damage Max Level General Information
Cygnus Bombards in a circling zone. 10 8
  • Scales with all stat bonuses
  • Area orbits clockwise and slightly further around the player
Zhar Ptytsia Bombards in a circling zone. 10 8
  • Scales with all stat bonuses
  • Area orbits counterclockwise around the player
Red Muscle Fires quickly in four fixed directions. 5 8
  • Ignores Duration
  • Shooting angles align slightly closer to vertical
Twice Upon a Time Fires quickly in four fixed directions. 5 8
  • Ignores Duration
  • Shooting angles align slightly closer to vertical
Flock Destroyer Summons capricious projectiles. Might interact with pickups. 10 8
  • Scales with all stat bonuses and Luck
  • Best with: Luck, Floor Chicken
  • Cats have unique interaction with Pickups
Horse Cartridge Aims at an enemy opposite of the faced direction. Stockpiles projectiles when enemies are out of range. 5 8
Levelin'Eh Duration affects Amount. Can deal critical damage. 20 8
  • Attacks in the same location as Santa Javelin, reflected across the vertical axis
  • Ignores Speed
Silver Sliver Counterpart to Shadow Servant and Ophion from Arcana I - Gemini 10 8
  • Scales with all stat bonuses and Luck
Party Pooper Counterpart to Party Popper from Arcana I - Gemini 5 8
  • Attacks behind the player
  • Scales with all stat bonuses
Silver Tongue Strikes enemies behind you. Damage multiplied by Recovery. 10 8
  • Attacks behind the player
  • Ignores Duration
Prism Damsel Has a small chance to freeze enemies. 8 8
  • Fires at opposite angles to Prism Lass
  • Best with Area
Speculo Globus Obtained only through Arcana I - Gemini 5 8
  • Fires behind the player
Speculo Raging Fire Obtained only through Arcana I - Gemini 10 8
  • Attacks are the vertical reflection of Raging Fire
Speculo Ice Fang Obtained only through Arcana I - Gemini 10 8
  • Attacks behind the player
Speculo Rock Riot Obtained only through Arcana I - Gemini 10 8
  • Attacks are the vertical reflection of Rock Riot
Speculo Gale Force Obtained only through Arcana I - Gemini 3 8
  • Attacks are the 180º rotation of Gale Force
Speculo Fulgur Obtained only through Arcana I - Gemini 10 8
  • Attacks behind the player
Speculo Keremet Bubbles Obtained only through Arcana I - Gemini 10 8
  • Attacks are the vertical reflection of Keremet Bubbles
Speculo Hex Obtained only through Arcana I - Gemini 10 8
  • Attacks are the vertical reflection of Hex
Speculo Refectio Obtained only through Arcana I - Gemini 3 8
  • Attacks are the 180º rotation of Refectio
Speculo Anura Summons frogs that damage enemies as they jump. 10 8
  • Attacks are below the player

Special Weapons

Special weapons cannot appear when leveling up. They are used as the structure of an attack that is temporary or an effect. When they are used as an effect, they behave slightly differently compared to when they are modified to be actual weapons.

Icon Weapon Base Damage General Information
Nduja Fritta Tanto 30
  • Pickup weapon
  • Scales with all stat bonuses
  • Duration affects both the Nduja Fritta Tanto and each individual flame
  • Area increases the growth rate of each flame
  • Affected by Gemini (I) Arcana, which cause an extra fire breath backwards.
Sorbetto 10
  • Pickup weapon
  • Scales with all stat bonuses and Luck (?)
  • Duration affects both the Sorbetto and each individual flame
  • Area increases the growth rate of each flame
  • Affected by Gemini (I) Arcana, which cause an extra fire breath backwards.
Dairy Cart 100
  • Environment weapon in Dairy Plant
  • Ignores Duration
  • Ignores Speed, Duration, Cooldown, and Amount as an effect
  • Does not bounce off the screen like Carréllo
  • Triggers when dealt enough damage.
  • Can kill Stalker, making it drops a Treasure Chest as an effect
Take Us Away 200
  • Environment weapon in Laborratory
  • Scales with all stat bonuses.
  • Gains bonus flat damage based on Area, Speed, and Duration.
Sarabande of Healing N/A
  • Arcana weapon
  • Ignores Might, Duration, and Speed
  • Ignores everything but Area as an effect
  • Deals damage equal to the amount healed as Arcana effect
Stained Glass 130
  • Environment weapon in Cappella Magna
  • Ignores Duration and Speed
  • Ignores Speed, Duration, Cooldown, and Amount as an effect
  • Triggers when dealt enough damage
  • Effect used by Game Killer (0),
Game Killer 10
  • Arcana weapon
  • Ignores Duration
  • Ignores Duration, Cooldown, and Amount as an effect
  • Shoot out a placeholder piano that aims straight at the closest enemy similar to Magic Wand
  • Deals damage equal to the experience gem value, multiplied by Growth
Heart of Fire 10
  • Arcana weapon
  • Ignores Duration and Speed
  • Ignores Speed, Duration, Cooldown, and Amount as an effect
  • Used by Gatti Amari when a cat consumes an Nduja Fritta Tanto
  • Retaliatory explosion scales with Armor as Arcana effect
  • Deals damage equal to its base damage or half the highest weapons Base Damage as Arcana effect, triggered by hitting enemies, taking damage, and destroying Light sources.
Out of Bounds 10
  • Arcana weapon
  • Ignores Duration and Speed
  • Ignores Duration, Speed, Cooldown, and Amount as an effect
  • Triggered by freezing enemies as Arcana effect,
Divine Bloodline N/A
  • Arcana weapon
  • Ignores Might, Area, Duration, and Speed
  • Ignores everything but Armor and deals contact damage as Arcana effect
Blood Astronomia 5
  • Arcana weapon
  • Ignores Area, Duration, and Speed
  • Scales with Magnet
  • Ignores Area, Duration, and Speed as Arcana effect
Wandering the Jet Black 10
  • Arcana weapon
  • Scales with Magnet and Recovery
Insatiable 10
  • Exclusive to  Je-Ne-Viv and  Victorian Horror (XVIII)
  • Ignores Duration, and Speed (and Area when not given by the Darkana)
  • Scales with Magnet and Greed
Acquazzone 10
  • Exclusive to  Ferryman and  Atlantis Shrine Wizard 
  • Ignores Area, Duration, Amount, and Speed
Shaft Orb
Burning Alcarde
Death Hand
Hand of Vlad
Ruler Sword Body
Bananastrophe
Sossoldi

Update history

This information has been manually compiled. Some updates may not be included yet.
  • update 17 August 2023 (Update):
    • Fixed hidden weapons not being checked when activating some arcanas.
    • Weapon Selection: Header size adjustment.
  • update 1 June 2023 (Update):
    • Fix for level up evo indicators not showing on passives for DLC weapons
    • Fix for issue where pick-up weapons could not be leveled up
  • update 21 July 2022 (Update):
    • Added a cap to Duration for Limit Broken weapons
    • Fixed some projectiles standing still when there are no enemies
  • update 12 April 2022 (Update):
    • Removed special BGM from weapons (it stays on special characters only)
  • update 25 March 2022 (Update):
    • Added info for discovered evolutions on Level Up panels
  • update 12 March 2022 (Update):
    • changes to cooldown don't reset weapon firing timers anymore
  • update 13 February 2022 (Update):
    • Minor tweaks to weapons rarity
  • update 16 January 2022 (Update):
    • after unlocking a handful of weapons, current stats and weapon levels will be displayed when leveling up and on the pause screen
  • update 7 January 2022 (Update):
    • tentative fix to slow downs with certain weapon combos
    • adjusted tips for a few weapons if there's any stat they ignore
  • update 27 October 2021 (Update):
    • Added to game.

Trivia

  • Before Ode to Castlevania DLC, Bi-Bracelet and Tri-Bracelet were the only evolutions that have a rarity of more than 1.
    • Fuwalafuwaloo used to have a rarity of 50, same as Vento Sacro; it changed to 1 in a later patch.
